E01.name: Merlin-1a
E01.text: If this craft returns to Earth, return this card to hand.

E02.name: Sterling Booster
E02.text: High reliability, single-use. Always available for purchase.

E03.name: Hydrogen Core
E03.text: Requires at least one Cryo Tank. High thrust.

E04.name: Ion Sustainer
E04.text: Low thrust but very reliable and efficient.

E05.name: Hybrid Cycle
E05.text: +1 Thrust while the rocket includes a Cryogenic tank. If this craft returns to Earth, return this card to hand.
E05.flavor: Well-balanced hybrid engine that burns almost anything.

E06.name: Raptor-X
E06.text: High risk, high reward. If this craft returns to Earth, return this card to hand.

E07.name: Kick Stage
E07.text: Stage: +2 Range for this launch. Still counts as your Engine for this mission. Discard after launch.
E07.flavor: A booster you drop once it has done its job.

T01.name: Standard Tank
T01.text: Compatible with most engines. Always available for purchase.

T03.name: Fuel Pod
T03.text: Stage: +1 Range for this launch. Discard after launch.

T04.name: Expandable Tank
T04.text: Stage: +2 Range for this launch. Discard after launch.
T04.flavor: Folds flat for cheap stacking — drop it when it's dry.

T05.name: Pressurized Tank
T05.text: Crew-rated. A craft carrying a Crewed payload must include a Pressurized tank.

P01.name: Comm Satellite
P01.text: Deploy in orbit: it stays on the board as a persistent communications satellite. Each Maintenance, you may spend 1 Energy on the satellite: gain 1 Credit if it is at Earth ZOI or closer, or 1 VP if beyond Earth ZOI. Once per round, when another player's craft moves beyond Earth ZOI, you may spend 1 Energy: gain 1 Credit from the bank (relay services).

P02.name: Imaging Probe
P02.text: Deploy in orbit or beyond: it stays on the board as a persistent science satellite. Each Maintenance, you may spend 1 Energy on the satellite: gain 1 Credit if it is at Earth ZOI or closer, or 1 VP if beyond Earth ZOI.

P03.name: Science Module
P03.text: Spend 2 Energy at Moon Orbit or beyond (once per round): gain 1 VP from research data, or 2 VP while a storm Event is active.

P04.name: Crew Capsule
P04.text: Spend 1 Energy when this capsule launches or relaunches from a surface. Enables crewed missions. If this craft returns to Earth, return this card to hand.

P05.name: CubeSat Cluster
P05.text: Cheap, small experiments. Remains on the board as an on-orbit asset.

P06.name: Landing Lander
P06.text: Enables surface landing. Alternative: use Rocket-as-Lander (Engine + sufficient Range).

P07.name: Cargo Return Capsule
P07.text: Useful for recovery or sample-return missions. If this craft returns to Earth, return this card to hand.

P08.name: Station Hub
P08.text: Core bus for a permanent orbital station. At High Orbit (GEO) with at least one Power, one LifeSupport, and one more Scientific or Electronics card attached, designate it an On-Orbit Station. Each Maintenance, you may spend 1 Energy on the station to gain 1 Credit from station operations.
P08.flavor: The anchor module for your first true station.

S01.name: Heat Shield
S01.text: Landing support. Use from a Sub-Orbital node to land safely. Discard after use. Always available for purchase.

S02.name: Recovery Chutes
S02.text: Landing support, Earth only (needs thick atmosphere). Use from Sub-Orbital Earth to land safely; after landing, gain 1 Credit from clean hardware recovery. Discard after use.

S03.name: Ceramic Tile Shield
S03.text: Landing support (Earth or Mars). Use from a Sub-Orbital node to land safely. When aerobraking with this card, it is not discarded (once per flight). If this craft returns to Earth, return this card to hand.
S03.flavor: A tougher shield for repeat flights.

S04.name: Guided Parafoil
S04.text: Landing support, Earth only. Use from Sub-Orbital Earth to land safely; after landing, gain 1 Credit from precision recovery. If this craft returns to Earth, return this card to hand.
S04.flavor: Steer it back for another launch.

M02.name: Lunar Flyby
M02.text:
Reach Moon Orbit and return to Earth.
Total route range needed: 10.

M03.name: Lunar Landing
M03.text:
Reach the Moon surface (one-way).
Have Landing Lander or Rocket-as-Lander.

M04.name: Mars Orbit Insertion
M04.text:
Reach Mars High Orbit.
Add Transfer Window cost to route plan.
Carry payload Mass 2+.

M05.name: Deep Space Probe
M05.text:
Reach Mars ZOI.
Add the current Transfer Window cost to your route plan.
Have a Scientific payload (Mass 2+) and a Sensor Array attached.
Spend 2 Energy for mission ops.

M06.name: Crewed Station Visit
M06.text:
Reach High Orbit (GEO), dock with any On-Orbit Station, and return to Earth.
Have a Crewed payload and a Docking support card on a craft with an Engine.

M07.name: Emergency Resupply
M07.text:
Reach LEO and return to Earth.
Carry payload Mass 2+.

M08.name: Science Relay
M08.text:
Reach High Orbit and return to Earth.
Have a Scientific or Electronics payload.
Spend 1 Energy to run relay instruments.

M09.name: Orbital Service Check
M09.text:
From LEO, reach High Orbit, then return to LEO.
Have a deployed Satellite in orbit; use a craft with an Engine and remaining Range.

M10.name: Capsule Recovery
M10.text:
From Sub-Orbital Earth, land at Earth.
Carry a payload (Mass 1+) and a Reentry support.

M11.name: Reusable Flight Test
M11.text:
Fly Earth → Sub-Orbital Earth → Earth.
Use a Reusable Payload and a Reusable Reentry support.

M12.name: Lunar Sample Return
M12.text:
Reach Moon surface, collect samples, and return to Earth.
Have Lander (or Rocket-as-Lander) + Cargo Return Capsule + a Reentry support for Earth landing.

C01.name: Reusable Refurb
C01.text: Your Reusable engines gain +1 Reliability. When you recover a Reusable card during Maintenance, gain 1 Credit.

C02.name: Cryo Handling
C02.text: Your rockets that include a Cryo Tank gain +1 Reliability on launch checks.

C03.name: Precision Guidance
C03.text: Increase mission success chance: +1 effective reliability on launch checks.

C04.name: Modular Payloads
C04.text: Reduce your payload's Mass by 1 (minimum 1) for Thrust checks.

EV01.name: Solar Storm
EV01.text: Global: All launches this round suffer -2 reliability.

EV02.name: Funding Boost
EV02.text: Occasional bonus: All players gain +3 Credits immediately.

EV03.name: Supply Delay
EV03.text: This round, the Launch New Craft action costs 1 Credit.

EV04.name: Tech Breakthrough
EV04.text: First player to launch this round reveals cards from the Component Deck until a Technology appears; add it to hand and discard the other revealed cards.

S05.name: Docking Adapter
S05.text: Enables docking with stations and other craft. Required for Docking missions. Spend 1 Energy to dock. If this craft returns to Earth, return this card to hand.

S06.name: Orbital Tug
S06.text: Enables docking. Spend 1 Energy when activating this craft in orbit to gain +1 Range. If this craft returns to Earth, return this card to hand.

EV05.name: Docking Opportunity
EV05.text: This round, any craft with a Docking tag that docks with an On-Orbit Station in High Orbit (GEO) gains +2 VP.

EV06.name: Transfer Window Storm
EV06.text: This round, Transfer Window cost is increased by +2 (max TW 5).

EV07.name: Launch Window
EV07.text: This round, Transfer Window cost is reduced by 2 (min TW 0).

EV08.name: Expanded Operations
EV08.text: This round, each player's hand limit is increased to 7.
EV08.flavor: Extra capacity for a busy quarter.

S07.name: Solar Panel
S07.text: Generates 2 Energy at the start of each Action Phase while this craft is in space. If this craft enters atmosphere, discard this card.

S08.name: RTG
S08.text: Generates 3 Energy at the start of each Action Phase. Works in any location. This card's Mass counts toward launch Thrust checks.
S08.flavor: Steady power far from the Sun.

S09.name: Battery Pack
S09.text: Single-use power cell. Discard at any time to add 2 Energy to its craft, anywhere (even in atmosphere or at launch). Provides no ongoing power.
S09.flavor: Crack it open when you need a jolt.

S10.name: Flight Computer
S10.text: Spend 1 Energy when this craft launches or relaunches from a surface; for that check, this craft gains +1 Reliability.
S10.flavor: A little more brain, a little less risk.

S11.name: Sensor Array
S11.text: Spend 1 Energy (once per round): gain 1 VP if a storm Event is active this round, or if this craft is at Sun Orbit or beyond. Required for any mission that requires Sensors.

S12.name: Habitation Ring
S12.text: Provides living quarters for long-duration crews. If attached to a Station Hub in High Orbit (GEO), it helps that craft qualify as an On-Orbit Station. While part of your On-Orbit Station: Crewed missions that dock there grant +1 VP.

S13.name: Microgravity Lab
S13.text: Orbital laboratory module that helps a craft qualify as an On-Orbit Station. Each Maintenance, if attached to a Station Hub in High Orbit (GEO), you may spend 1 Energy to gain 1 VP from station research.
S13.flavor: Science works better above the world.

M13.name: Tourist Hop
M13.text:
Fly Earth → Sub-Orbital Earth → Earth.
Carry a Crewed payload and a Reentry support.
M13.flavor: Ten minutes of weightlessness, a lifetime of bragging.

M14.name: Weather Satellite
M14.text: Deploy a Satellite payload at High Orbit (GEO).

M15.name: Sounding Flight
M15.text:
Fly Earth → Sub-Orbital Earth → Earth.
Carry a Scientific payload and spend 1 Energy for flight science.

M16.name: Lunar Orbiter
M16.text: Deploy a Satellite payload at Moon Orbit.
M16.flavor: A steady voice around the Moon.

M17.name: Crewed Lunar Flyby
M17.text:
Reach Moon Orbit and return to Earth.
Carry a Crewed payload and a Reentry support.
M17.flavor: The far side, with your own eyes.

M18.name: Station Assembly
M18.text:
Designate an On-Orbit Station at High Orbit (GEO).
Completes when your station qualifies: Station Hub + Power + LifeSupport + one more Scientific or Electronics card.
M18.flavor: Bolt by bolt, a home above the world.

M19.name: Mars Landing
M19.text:
Reach Mars Surface.
Add the current Transfer Window cost to your route plan.
Have a Lander payload or Rocket-as-Lander.

M20.name: Asteroid Rendezvous
M20.text:
Reach Sun Orbit.
Have a Scientific payload (Mass 2+) and a Sensor Array.
Spend 2 Energy for survey ops.

EV09.name: Solar Flare Watch
EV09.text: This round, all launches suffer -1 Reliability, and Sensor Array activations grant double rewards.
EV09.flavor: Bad day to fly, great day for science.

EV10.name: Broadcast Rights
EV10.text: This round, income abilities on Satellite and Station assets pay +1 Credit.

EV11.name: Media Frenzy
EV11.text: This round, completed Prestige missions grant +1 VP.

EV12.name: Comms Blackout
EV12.text: This round, a player may complete missions only if they control a deployed Electronics asset — or pay 1 Credit to a player who does.

EV13.name: Stranded Crew
EV13.text: Place a marker at LEO. The first craft with a Crewed payload to visit LEO and then return to Earth rescues the crew: its owner gains 5 VP. This effect persists until claimed.
EV13.flavor: Somebody up there needs a ride home.

C05.name: Trajectory Planning
C05.text: Your craft treat the Transfer Window cost as 1 lower (minimum 0).
C05.flavor: Leave exactly when the math says.

C06.name: Launch Abort System
C06.text: When one of your Reliability checks fails, you may pay 2 Credits to reroll it once.
C06.flavor: Push the big red button, try again.

C07.name: Contracting Office
C07.text: When you complete a Commercial mission, gain +1 Credit.
C07.flavor: Somebody has to read the fine print.

C08.name: Mass Production
C08.text: Basic cards cost you 1 less Credit (minimum 1).

C09.name: Staging Simulations
C09.text: Your Stage bonuses grant +1 additional Range.
C09.flavor: Practice the drop a thousand times.

C10.name: Deep Space Network
C10.text: Your deployed assets beyond Earth ZOI have +1 Power.
C10.flavor: Giant ears listening to the dark.

P09.name: Rover
P09.text: Deploy on a Moon or Mars surface: it stays as a persistent surface asset. Each Maintenance, you may spend 1 Energy to gain 1 VP from surface science.

P10.name: Space Telescope
P10.text: Deploy at High Orbit (GEO) or beyond. Each Maintenance, you may spend 1 Energy to gain 1 VP (2 VP while a storm Event is active).

P11.name: Fuel Depot
P11.text: Deploy in orbit. Once per round, a craft at the same node may spend 1 of this depot's Energy to gain +2 Range.
P11.flavor: Fill up before the long haul.

S14.name: Landing Legs
S14.text: Propulsive landings by this craft cost no extra Range. Landing still requires an Engine.

S15.name: Basic Battery
S15.text: Single-use power cell. Discard at any time to add 1 Energy to its craft, anywhere (even in atmosphere or at launch). Its Mass counts toward launch Thrust checks. Always available for purchase.
S15.flavor: Heavy, cheap, and it always works.

P12.name: Light Payload
P12.text: A plain payload with no special ability. Satisfies missions that require carrying an (uncrewed) payload. Always available for purchase.
P12.flavor: Cheap mass with a mission patch.

P13.name: Standard Payload
P13.text: A plain payload. Its heavier build satisfies missions that require payload Mass 2+. Always available for purchase.
P13.flavor: A little more heft for the manifest.

P14.name: Heavy Payload
P14.text: A plain heavy payload. Satisfies payload Mass 3 requirements and stresses your Thrust budget. Always available for purchase.
P14.flavor: Somebody has to lift the big one.

M21.name: Suborbital Test Flight
M21.text:
Fly Earth → Sub-Orbital Earth → Earth and land safely.
No payload required.
Standing contract: always available; each agency may complete it once per game.
M21.flavor: Light the candle, get it back in one piece.
